extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. Transition [t34] sets sent:=true on send_attempt when (opted_out and stage>=10), directly producing a state with opted_out=true and sent=true. This is reachable: opted_out is set true by [t1] (optout) and is never reset to false anywhere; stage advances 0..10 as a combination lock where exactly one key advances each stage (0->1 key_0[t3], 1->2 key_2[t6], 2->3 key_2[t9], 3->4 key_0[t12], 4->5 key_1[t15], 5->6 key_0[t18], 6->7 key_0[t21], 7->8 key_2[t24], 8->9 key_2[t27], 9->10 key_0[t30]); these key guards depend only on stage, not on opted_out, and each sets sent:=false (harmless). Firing optout first, then the 10 advancing keys to reach stage=10, then send_attempt triggers [t34] (its guard opted_out and stage>=10 is enabled; note [t33] for stage<10 is no longer applicable and [t2] is disabled because opted_out is true). The 12-event trace optout, key_0, key_2, key_2, key_0, key_1, key_0, key_0, key_2, key_2, key_0, send_attempt is within the 13-event bound and ends in opted_out=true, sent=true, stage=10, violating the invariant.
